name: Pistachio, Rose & Raspberry Madeleines
intro: The thing about madeleines is that they should ideally be served still warm from then oven, which gives them a melt-in-the-mouth quality that makes them highly addictive. If you can stop after eating just one madeleine, then, believe me, you haven’t had a good madeleine yet. These are perfumed with the scent of Persian roses, nutty with pistachios and topped with a raspberry for a little juicy burst. I defy you to not scoff at least three of them in a row.

ingredients.item: unsalted butter, melted and cooled, plus 50g (1¾oz) extra, softened, for greasing

method: Crack the eggs into a large mixing bowl, add the caster sugar and beat until pale and fluffy. In a separate bowl, combine the flour, baking powder and salt and set aside. Mix the cooled melted butter with the honey and rosewater and pour this into the egg-and-sugar mixture. Stir until the mixture is smooth.

Add the chopped pistachios to the wet ingredients and stir in well. Lastly, incorporate half of the flour into the mixture, give it a vigorous stir, then add the remaining flour and stir until all the flour is evenly combined. Cover the bowl with clingfilm and refrigerate for at least 2 hours, or overnight if preferred.

Once you are ready to bake, preheat the oven to 190°C/fan 170°C/gas mark 5. Brush each mould in a 12-shell madeleine tin with softened butter and place it in the freezer to harden for a few minutes, then brush again with softened butter. Put 1 heaped tablespoon of the batter into each mould. You won’t use up all the batter in one go, so refrigerate the rest of the batter until the first batch is cooked. Push a single raspberry into the batter in the centre of each mould. Bake for 5 minutes, turn the oven off for 1 minute, then set the oven to 160°C/fan 140°C/gas mark 3 and bake for a further 5 minutes, or until they are golden all over.

Remove the madeleines from the oven and turn them out (a teaspoon will help you to pop them out of their moulds). Wash the madeline tin and repeat the process until the rest of the batter is used up. Serve the madeleines hot from the oven, although they can be kept for 48 hours in an airtight container – but bear in mind that they always taste best freshly baked.
